source.title: Understanding the Role of Equivariance in Self-supervised Learning

confidence_rationale: Wang et al. establish that invariant SSL architectures (JEPA family) systematically discard features required for equivariant downstream tasks, with an information-theoretic bound on the information loss. This directly supports Q-014: JEPA invariances create blind spots for downstream tasks that require sensitivity to the suppressed features. In REE attribution contexts, those suppressed features may include precisely the event-type distinctions required for causal harm attribution.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Invariant SSL methods discard augmentation-related information through the invariance mechanism itself; equivariant SSL recovers this via an explaining-away synergy; the information gap is measurable and systematically affects performance on downstream tasks requiring the suppressed features.
mapping.ree_translation: JEPA invariances trained to suppress perceptual nuisance variation will also suppress event-type distinctions that happen to look like nuisance variation at the feature level. In REE attribution -- where the agent must distinguish between superficially similar trajectories with different causal structures (agent-caused vs. environment-caused harm, for example) -- this information loss constitutes an ethical blind spot: the architecture structurally cannot represent the relevant distinction, not merely as a capacity limitation but as a design consequence of the invariance objective.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Wang et al. study visual augmentations (crop, color jitter, rotation) in static image classification. The information discarded is visual transformation information. Whether the analogous information in a temporal dynamics model -- fine-grained event-type distinctions required for moral attribution -- is also suppressed by JEPA's invariance objective has not been directly tested. The argument by analogy is plausible but requires validation in the temporal sequential domain.
mapping.source_context: Image-domain self-supervised learning, ViT backbone, standard vision benchmarks, downstream classification and equivariant prediction tasks.

confidence_components.notes: NeurIPS 2024, rigorous theoretical and empirical treatment. Confidence above baseline because the mechanism is general (invariance = information loss) and the mapping to JEPA-based E1/E2 in REE is direct in principle, even if not empirically validated in that specific context. Transfer risk is moderate: the visual augmentation domain is quite different from temporal dynamics.
